channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Video shows police respond to active shooter at FSU
item.description: Video taken by a student, Ryan Cedergren, shows the moment police arrived on the scene as people fled the student union building.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, NewsNation

item.title: Arson attack probe at Pennsylvania governor's mansion looking into suspect's hatred of Josh Shapiro
item.description: HERSHEY, Pa. (AP) — Authorities investigating why a man set fire to Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s official residence are probing whether the suspect was motivated by the Democrat's Jewish faith or positions on Israel’s war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about an active shooter alert at Florida State University
item.description: Authorities say at least six people have been hospitalized after a shooting at Florida State University.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Student who shot 4 at Dallas high school was targeting specific student, school police chief says
item.description: The chief of police for the Dallas Independent School District says the student who shot four students when he opened fire at a Dallas high school this week was in and out of the building in less than two minutes and is believed to have been targeting a specific student.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Stengle, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Wrong man, wrong bed, wrong treatment: Patient's death prompts medical board to discipline California doctor
item.description: A California physician has been placed on five years’ probation by the state's medical board for administering a course of treatment to a patient that was intended for the patient in an adjacent hospital bed.
item.dc:creator: Robert Price, Fox 5

item.title: Officials demand answers as crews work to restore power after another Puerto Rico blackout
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Crews scrambled to restore power to Puerto Rico on Thursday after a blackout hit the entire island, affecting the main international airport, hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: El Paso mom honors son during National Donate Life Month
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) — April is National Donate Life Month, dedicated to raising awareness of donating organs and to thank those who have donated. Heyma Chavez, an El Paso mother, is honoring her son Ivan Bustamante, who died and donated his organs to three people in need.
item.dc:creator: Nigel Wickens, NewsNation

item.title: Russia strikes Ukrainian city of Dnipro as Kyiv seeks security pledges in Paris
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian forces launched a massive drone attack on the southeastern Ukrainian city of Dnipro, killing three people, including a child, and wounding dozens, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Louisiana child killer spared by Biden faces death penalty again after ‘outrageous’ commutation: DA
item.description: Child killer Thomas Sanders, who was convicted and sentenced to death in 2010, is facing death again after former President Joe Biden commuted his sentence in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Red Robin launches $20 Bottomless Burger Pass for National Burger Month
item.description: May is National Burger Month and Red Robin is celebrating with the launch of its Bottomless Burger Pass — a limited-time offer that gives fans a month of daily gourmet burgers and bottomless sides for just $20. The pass will be available for purchase beginning April 17.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Visa cancellations sow panic for international students, with more than 1,000 fearing deportation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — At first, the bar association for immigration attorneys began receiving inquiries from a couple students a day. These were foreigners studying in the U.S., and they'd discovered in early April their legal status had been terminated with little notice.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, MAKIYA SEMINERA and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Actor Haley Joel Osment booked for 'public intoxication' at California ski resort
item.description: Actor Haley Joel Osment, famous for his role as the child who saw “dead people” in the 1999 thriller “The Sixth Sense” starring Bruce Willis, was reportedly arrested at a ski resort in Mammoth Lakes for public intoxication, according to multiple media reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WGN 9

item.title: North Carolina teenager, aspiring truck mechanic, killed on way to senior prom
item.description: A teen in North Carolina was killed on Saturday in a car crash after he initially wasn't going to the event. NC State Highway Patrol troopers said speed was a factor in the crash.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kamala Harris holds lead in hypothetical California governor race, poll shows
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris would enjoy a wide lead over the rest of the field if she were to enter the 2026 race for California governor, the results of a recent poll show.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Moderate Republicans draw red line on Medicaid cuts in Trump agenda bill
item.description: Vulnerable and moderate House Republicans are warning House GOP leaders that they will not support the “one big, beautiful bill” encompassing President Trump’s legislative agenda if it includes cuts to Medicaid benefits.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: New York family told missing woman was found dead, only to learn she's living in Detroit
item.description: A New York family is navigating an emotional journey after learning their sister, whom officials declared dead last year, is actually alive and living out of state.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Garcia, ABC 4

item.title: Noem announces cancellation of $2.7 million in grants to Harvard
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on Wednesday announced the cancellation of more than $2.7 million in grants to Harvard University amid tensions between the school and the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, ABC 4

item.title: Officials in Puerto Rico demand answers after another blackout leaves 1.4 million without power
item.description: Crews worked early Thursday to restore power to Puerto Rico after a blackout across the entire island that affected the main international airport, several hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, KRON4

item.title: DOGE targets a community service program in its latest cost-cutting effort
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — A 30-year-old community service program that sends young adults to work on projects across the U.S. was the latest target of the Trump administration 's campaign to slash government spending.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Handyman named person of interest after missing woman's body found buried in concrete
item.description: A missing woman was found buried and partially encased in concrete more than a week after she went missing. Now, deputies are questioning her handyman for his possible role in her disappearance and death.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Temu and Shein say they're raising prices due to tariffs
item.description: Both companies said they would be making “price adjustments” starting April 25, although neither provided details about the size of the increases.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tariffs will cost families nearly $5k per year, Kentucky governor says
item.description: "That's months upon months upon months of groceries. That's months upon months of rent. That might be your entire annual deductible if you've got private health insurance coverage," he said.
item.dc:creator: Isaiah Calalang, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lost toddler found miles from home by dog that guided him to safety, sheriff says
item.description: When an Arizona toddler wandered away from home Monday, a team of deputies, search and rescue volunteers and rangers began their search. In the end, it was a protective pup who track down the 2-year-old.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Zoom service restored after outage affects thousands
item.description: Thousands of Zoom users reported issues accessing virtual meetings Wednesday, with the site confirming shortly after noon it was investigating the issue.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Student accused in Dallas school shooting walked toward group in hall and opened fire, injuring 4
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The student accused of injuring four in a shooting at a Dallas high school was let into the building through an unsecured door and then walked down a hallway toward a group of students, opening fire on them and then appearing to take a point-blank shot at one, according to an arrest
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wall Street banker washes up dead on paradise beach weeks after disappearing on vacation
item.description: The family of a missing New York banker confirmed that the remains that washed ashore on a beach in Spain belong to the 37-year-old, who had vanished nearly a month ago during vacation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ACLU sues Defense Department school system over banning race- and gender-related books
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union has sued the Department of Defense’s school system for children of military families, asserting that the removal of race- and gender-related books and curricula violated students’ First Amendment protections against government censorship.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US measles cases surpass 700 with outbreaks in six states. Here's what to know
item.description: Kansas health officials confirmed five new cases of measles Wednesday in an outbreak in the southwest corner of the state that's linked to Texas and New Mexico. Last week, U.S. measles cases topped 700 as Indiana joined five others states with active outbreaks.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Newsom says California will sue over Trump tariffs
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom and Attorney General Rob Bonta announced plans to sue the Trump administration over recent tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Olympic event to be played in front of iconic movie backdrop at 2028 Games, organizers say
item.description: LA28 officials have announced a where that a first-time Olympic sport will be played at a famed Hollywood location.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, Fox 5

item.title: White House removes wire spot from press pool
item.description: The White House is making changes to which outlets are included in the press pool covering President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Starbucks introducing new dress code for baristas, a move protested by some
item.description: Baristas at your local Starbucks will soon be sporting a new, more consistent look after the company announced a new dress code this week.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, WGN 9

item.title: Maryland senator to visit El Salvador to check on mistakenly deported man
item.description: Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D) said he is traveling to El Salvador to check on the status of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a deported legal resident.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Wink Martindale, game show host and Elvis Presley interviewer, dies at 91
item.description: Wink Martindale, the host of game shows who also did one of the first recorded television interviews with a young Elvis Presley, has died at 91.
item.dc:creator: By BETH HARRIS Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Man in tactical gear attacks officers with pepper spray at Boston-area courthouse, video shows
item.description: Police in a Boston suburb have arrested a man who they say entered a courthouse while wearing a gas mask and tactical gear and attacked several people with pepper spray.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Jury is picked for Karen Read's retrial over death of police boyfriend, opening statements next week
item.description: The jury has been picked in Karen Read’s second murder trial over the death of her police officer boyfriend, with opening statements scheduled for next week. An 18-member panel — nine men and nine women — was chosen by Tuesday. There will be 12 jurors and six alternates.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden alleges Trump has 'taken a hatchet' to Social Security in his first post-presidency speech
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Former President Joe Biden warned on Tuesday that President Donald Trump has “taken a hatchet” to Social Security, weighing in on a critical issue for millions of Americans as the 82-year-old Democrat briefly returned to the national stage.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, Fox 5
